The boxer is famous for his power.
He fights with an aggressive style.
His punches hit with great force.
Fans always watch his matches.
The first match was very intense.
He landed one hundred twenty punches.
That number was the starting point.
The second match followed soon after.
He improved his performance greatly.
His punch count increased by twenty five percent.
Twenty five percent of one hundred twenty is thirty.
So he landed one hundred fifty punches.
The third match was the final one.
He added thirty more punches to the total.
One hundred fifty plus thirty equals one hundred eighty.
That was the count for the third fight.
Now we must add all the numbers.
The first match had one hundred twenty.
The second match had one hundred fifty.
The third match had one hundred eighty.
Adding the first two gives two hundred seventy.
Two hundred seventy plus one hundred eighty is four hundred fifty.
The total count is four hundred fifty punches.
This total covers all three matches.
The boxer showed great improvement.
His power grew with each fight.
The crowd cheered for his strength.
Every punch was counted carefully.
The math is simple to verify.
One hundred twenty plus one hundred fifty is correct.
One hundred fifty plus one hundred eighty is correct.
The final sum is indeed four hundred fifty.
He is a true heavyweight champion.
His legacy is built on power.
